title: PayrollSubmitResult v1
description: Result of a target-portal submit Service Task. Carries totals reconciliation, the per-employee row diff (roster_diff) that drives BPM lifecycle subprocesses, and portal-issued submission proof.

clientIdentifier.description: Echoed from the submit-request for shared-creds and per-client portals; absent for per-firm portals.

portalConfirmation.description: Portal-issued submission proof (CCSS planilla id, INS contribution id, Hacienda submission ref). Null when submission did not reach a confirmation.

grandTotal.description: Post-submit grand total visible on the portal (sum of all salaries, changed and unchanged). Reconciles against the sum of all salaries pulled from the source-of-truth; any drift flips the run to MISMATCH, with rosterDiff naming the employees that explain the gap.

updatedCount.description: Diagnostic: how many rows the agent actually changed on this submit. Not used for reconciliation.

expected.description: Sum of every canonical-input salary the run intended to put on the portal. Surfaced as a process variable for BPMN gating: 'expected != grandTotal' (or 'expected != portalReported' on portals that distinguish working from reported totals) is the deterministic mismatch signal. Optional; older capture-only adapters omit it.

portalReported.description: Portal's reported/saved salaries total — distinct from grandTotal on portals that distinguish a working/draft total from a saved/reported total (CCSS Sicere's 'Total de Salarios Reportados'). Optional; portals without that distinction leave it null.

submittedRows.description: Rows the agent actually filled and submitted. One entry per matched canonical employee.

roster_diff.description: Per-target reconciliation. Drives BPM lifecycle subprocesses (register hires, deregister terminations) when status=PARTIAL.

missingFromPortal.description: In source payroll, not found on target portal. Each entry is a likely new-hire registration candidate.

missingFromPayroll.description: On target portal, not in source payroll. Each entry is a likely deregistration candidate. Confirm before acting — terminations are high-risk.

errorDetail.description: Populated when status=FAILED. Engineering escalation path uses this to triage.

errorClass.description: Either a coarse classification (TRANSIENT|PERMANENT) or the fully-qualified Java class of the originating component, depending on producer.

category.description: Adapter-emitted categories follow the §15.5 vocabulary: CREDENTIALS_INVALID (login rejected), CREDENTIALS_EXPIRED (login forced password change), PORTAL_UNREACHABLE (network/DNS/timeout), PORTAL_AUTH_BLOCKED (CAPTCHA / step-up / anti-bot), UNEXPECTED (anything else). Listener-side categories: DUPLICATE_ENVELOPE (warm-cache duplicate, normally re-publishes the cached result instead), EXPIRED_DUPLICATE (cold-cache duplicate per SqsMigrationPlan.md §6.6 Q6b — receiveCount>1 with no cached result, e.g. worker restart between original receive and redelivery), UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION, SCHEMA_VIOLATION. Pre-§15 worker-side categories (NETWORK, SELECTOR, CREDENTIALS, PORTAL_REJECTED, OTHER) kept for backwards compatibility. New adapters should use the §15.5 values.

review.description: Populated for MISMATCH/PARTIAL runs. Renders in the Praxis HITL task form: signals shown grouped by type, allowedActions rendered as buttons.

canonical.description: Canonical-side amount for TOTAL_GAP signals. Money — accepts JSON string (canonical wire form) or JSON number (Jackson default for BigDecimal); both equivalent on the wire.

portal.description: Portal-side amount for TOTAL_GAP signals. Money — string or number.

gap.description: portal - canonical, signed. Money — string or number.

name.description: Canonical (source-of-truth) employee name. For NAME_DRIFT, paired with portalName to surface the disagreement.

portalName.description: Portal-side employee name. Populated for NAME_DRIFT signals so HITL can compare against the canonical name.

expectedSalary.description: Expected salary for MISSING_FROM_PORTAL signals. Money — string or number.
